title: Thelypteris costata
intro: Thelypteris costata, also known as the flat shield fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and improve immune function. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris costata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Thelypteris costata is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and provide relief from a variety of ailments.
taxonomy: Thelypteris costata is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae. This species of fern is part of the genus Thelypteris, which comprises various species of evergreen ferns. Thelypteris costata belongs to the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and fern allies. The classification of Thelypteris costata also includes the order Polypodiales, a large and diverse order of ferns. At the kingdom level, Thelypteris costata is classified as Plantae.
uses: Thelypteris costata is known to have various medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions in some parts of the world. In folk medicine, it is often used to make teas or infusions that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Some studies have shown that extracts from the plant may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which could contribute to its potential health benefits. However, more studies are necessary to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential side effects.

parts: Thelypteris costata, also known as the mountain shield fern, is a plant species native to Central and South America.
